nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and inshore reef areas; tends to inhabit coral or rock outcroppings situated in sandy areas at depths of 10–90 m

diet.notes: Has been documented accepting a variety of foods including frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, dried seaweed, and high-quality flake or pellet foods. Marine algae-based foods are recommended as a regular part of the diet.

summary: The Tuxedo Damselfish is a small reef fish recognizable by its white body and three bold black vertical bands. It is commonly considered a good choice for beginner marine aquarists, though it tends to become territorial and aggressive as an adult and does best with robust tankmates in a well-decorated aquarium.
careNotes: The Tuxedo Damselfish, sometimes encountered in older trade literature under the name Chrysiptera tricincta, is a small reef fish from the lagoon and inshore reef zones of the Western Pacific. It is commonly considered a good choice for beginner marine aquarists, and the species generally accepts a wide variety of aquarium foods and conditions without difficulty. That said, the Tuxedo Damselfish tends to become increasingly territorial and assertive with age, and its aggression toward other tank inhabitants should not be underestimated. An aquarium furnished with plenty of live rock and hiding places gives each fish the opportunity to establish its own space, which tends to reduce conflict. Despite its assertiveness, the species is generally considered reef-safe and has not been reported to harm corals or disturb aquascaping. Males tend to be slightly larger than females, though visual sexing is otherwise difficult. Juveniles may display somewhat more subdued coloration than fully developed adults.
breedingNotes: The Tuxedo Damselfish is oviparous, reproducing by forming pairs and laying adhesive eggs directly on a hard surface. The male takes responsibility for guarding and aerating the eggs until they hatch. Captive breeding has been reported but is considered relatively uncommon among hobbyists.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Direct species page found via search; the collection URL supplied in the manifest did not list this species directly.

additional.name: BlueZoo Aquatics
additional.notes: Confirmed: 2.5 in adult size, 30 gal minimum tank, aggressive temperament, reef safe, beginner care level. Salinity and temperature parameters not explicitly stated.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: FishBase redirects Chrysiptera tricincta to Amblypomacentrus tricinctus (Allen & Randall, 1974), confirming the current valid scientific name. Wild temperature range 24.3–28.9°C; adult size 6.0 cm TL; western Pacific distribution from Indonesia to Samoa.

additional.name: SaltwaterFish.com
additional.notes: Confirmed 30 gal minimum tank, 74–78°F temperature, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, reef safe, beginner level. Lifespan stated as 5–7 years. Adult size given as up to 4 inches (slightly higher than other sources).

additional.name: Quality Marine
additional.notes: Confirmed: ~3 in adult size, 30 gal minimum, 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, territorial/aggressive temperament, reef safe, beginner level.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous. Males guard and aerate eggs laid on a hard substrate. Captive breeding has been reported but is not common among hobbyists.
